```{r}

# fit_reappraised_recalc.R
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fit both `reappraised` and `recalc` to a small set of baseline two-group
# comparisons (reported means, SDs, ns, and a two-sided p), where the reported
# p-values were *truncated* to 3 dp. Both tools ask the same question — is the
# reported p compatible with the summary statistics? — but express it
# differently:
#   * recalc      -> a per-study multiverse interval + an in-bounds flag
#                    (here with rounding = "truncate" to match the report).
#   * reappraised -> a per-study single recalculated p + its distance from the
#                    reported p (pval_cont_fn(); it assumes half-up rounding).
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(recalc)
  library(reappraised)
})

dat <- structure(list(m1 = c(0.05, 0.06, 0.04, -0.06, 0.03),
                      m2 = c(0, -0.07, -0.05, 0, -0.08),
                      sd1 = c(0.96, 1.11, 1.03, 0.99, 1.22),
                      sd2 = c(1.07, 0.83, 1.02, 0.99, 1.3),
                      n1 = c(25L, 25L, 25L, 25L, 25L),
                      n2 = c(25L, 25L, 25L, 25L, 25L),
                      p = c(0.816, 0.595, 0.712, 0.785, 0.721)),
                 row.names = c(NA, -5L), class = "data.frame")

# Reporting precision (means/SDs to 2 dp, p to 3 dp). Values like 0 and 1.3 are
# treated as 0.00 / 1.30 to match the 2-dp convention of the rest of the table.
m_digits <- 2L; sd_digits <- 2L; p_digits <- 3L
rounding_map <- c(trunc = "truncate", truncate = "truncate",
                  half_up = "half_up", bankers = "bankers")

# ---- recalc -----------------------------------------------------------------
# Per study: does the reported p fall inside recalc's t-test p-multiverse
# (Student + Welch, two-sided), given the reported summary stats and rounding?
recalc_fit <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(nrow(dat)), function(i) {
  res <- suppressWarnings(recalc_independent_t_p(
    m1 = dat$m1[i], m2 = dat$m2[i], sd1 = dat$sd1[i], sd2 = dat$sd2[i],
    n1 = dat$n1[i], n2 = dat$n2[i],
    m_digits = m_digits, sd_digits = sd_digits,
    p = dat$p[i], p_digits = p_digits, p_operator = "equals",
    alternative = "two.sided"
  ))
  rp <- res$reproduced
  data.frame(
    study            = i,
    p_reported       = dat$p[i],
    recalc_min_p     = round(rp$min_p, 4),
    recalc_max_p     = round(rp$max_p, 4),
    recalc_inbounds  = rp$p_inbounds,        # default: gap-aware union
    recalc_inbounds_hull = rp$p_inbounds_hull,
    covered_fraction = round(rp$covered_fraction, 3)
  )
}))

# ---- reappraised ------------------------------------------------------------
# Build the load_clean()-style wide frame pval_cont_fn() expects: groups encoded
# as numeric suffixes (m1/m2, s1/s2, n1/n2), decimal places in dpm*/dps*/dpp,
# the reported p as a string in `p` plus numeric `p_num`, and data_type tag.
reapp_df <- data.frame(
  study = paste0("S", seq_len(nrow(dat))), var = "baseline",
  n1 = dat$n1, n2 = dat$n2,
  m1 = dat$m1, m2 = dat$m2,
  s1 = dat$sd1, s2 = dat$sd2,
  p = formatC(dat$p, format = "f", digits = p_digits), p_num = dat$p,
  dpm1 = m_digits, dpm2 = m_digits, dps1 = sd_digits, dps2 = sd_digits,
  dpp = p_digits,
  data_type = c("pval_cont", rep(NA_character_, nrow(dat) - 1L)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

reapp <- pval_cont_fn(df = reapp_df, calc_refp = "n", verbose = FALSE)
rr <- reapp$pval_cont_results
# pval_cont_fn also returns a per-study min/max p range (over the rounding
# preimage AND equal/unequal variance: the "either.var" columns) plus a flag,
# directly comparable to recalc's interval. Note reappraised assumes *half-up*
# symmetric rounding, whereas recalc here uses the matched "truncate".
reapp_fit <- data.frame(
  study           = seq_len(nrow(rr)),
  p_reported      = rr$reported_pvalue_numeric,
  reapp_calc_p    = round(rr$pvalue_calc_from_summary_stats, 4),
  reapp_min_p     = round(rr$`min_possible_p_either.var`, 4),
  reapp_max_p     = round(rr$`max_possible_p_either.var`, 4),
  reapp_inbounds  = rr$`pvalue_flag_either.var` != "flag",  # "" = consistent
  diff_rep_calc_p = round(rr$difference_reported_calculated_p, 4)
)

# ---- report -----------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n================ recalc (rounding = half-up or bankers) ================\n")
print(recalc_fit, row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n================ reappraised (pval_cont_fn; half-up rounding) =\n")
print(reapp_fit, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n================ side-by-side ================================\n")
comparison <- data.frame(
  study          = recalc_fit$study,
  p_reported     = recalc_fit$p_reported,
  recalc_range   = sprintf("[%.3f, %.3f]", recalc_fit$recalc_min_p, recalc_fit$recalc_max_p),
  recalc_ok      = recalc_fit$recalc_inbounds,
  reapp_range    = sprintf("[%.3f, %.3f]", reapp_fit$reapp_min_p, reapp_fit$reapp_max_p),
  reapp_ok       = reapp_fit$reapp_inbounds
)
print(comparison, row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(recalc_fit, "fit_recalc.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(reapp_fit,  "fit_reappraised.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(comparison, "fit_comparison.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("\nSaved fit_recalc.csv, fit_reappraised.csv, fit_comparison.csv\n")

```
